regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect light east-southeast winds at 12 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. By the third day, winds will shift to south-southwest at 14 knots accompanied by rain showers and a 37% rain probability, which could impact both Miami and Port Everglades operations and excursions. Ports farther north and west remain clear with lower rain chances and lighter winds, maintaining stable conditions for cruise operations. Passengers should monitor updates as increasing rain chances and shifting winds could influence itinerary adjustments beyond 48 hours.

Overall, the moderate wind speeds and low rain chances suggest stable sea conditions for transit between the Bahamas and Central Caribbean ports through this period. Passengers and crew should monitor updates for any changes as the drizzle onset approaches beyond 48 hours.
